ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon-style webshell — IIS worker spawning interactive children or writing .aspx to Exchange virtual paths
ucs.description: Hunts the signature post-exploitation step Silk Typhoon (Microsoft = HAFNIUM, Mandiant = UNC2639/UNC2640-adjacent) used after chaining the Exchange ProxyLogon CVEs (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and continues to use against on-prem Exchange and other internet-facing edge appliances: the IIS worker process w3wp.exe drops or executes a China Chopper-style ASPX webshell (e.g. supp0rt.aspx, OutlookEN.aspx, help.aspx, RedirSuiteServiceProxy.aspx) under Exchange virtual directories and then spawns cmd/powershell/net/whoami for hands-on-keyboard recon. Pairing the unusual w3wp.exe child with the
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","7z.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\*") by host Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — IIS worker spawning interactive children OR dropping ASPX webshell
let _exch_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\"
]);
let _suspect_children = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe",
    "hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe",
    "reg.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe",
    "ping.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe"
]);
let ChildProcs =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ (_suspect_children)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildImage = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Signal = "w3wp_spawns_interactive_child";
let WebshellWrites =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_exch_paths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildImage = FileName, ChildCmd = FolderPath,
              Signal = "w3wp_writes_aspx_to_exchange_vdir";
union ChildProcs, WebshellWrites
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — IIS worker spawning interactive children (4688) OR ASPX writes (Sysmon EID 11)
let _suspect_children = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe",
    "hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe",
    "reg.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe",
    "ping.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe"
]);
let _exch_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\"
]);
let ChildProcs =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where tolower(ParentProcessName) endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in (_suspect_children)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              Signal = "w3wp_spawns_interactive_child";
let WebshellWrites =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 11
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             TargetFilename = tostring(EventData.TargetFilename),
             ActorUser = tostring(EventData.User)
    | where tolower(Image) endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetFilename endswith ".aspx" or TargetFilename endswith ".ashx" or TargetFilename endswith ".asmx"
    | where TargetFilename has_any (_exch_paths)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = ActorUser,
              NewProcessName = TargetFilename, CommandLine = Image,
              Signal = "w3wp_writes_aspx_to_exchange_vdir";
union ChildProcs, WebshellWrites
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon's hallmark, well-documented in Microsoft's Mar-2021 ProxyLogon disclosure and 2024-2025 IT-supply-chain reporting, is dropping a China Chopper-style ASPX shell into Exchange virtual directories (aspnet_client, owa/auth, ecp/auth) and driving it via w3wp.exe-spawned cmd/powershell. Generic 'webshell' rules miss the actor; this UC is anchored on (a) the IIS worker as parent and (b) the

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS credential theft via comsvcs.dll MiniDump (post-Exchange foothold)
ucs.description: Microsoft and Volexity reporting on HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon post-ProxyLogon activity called out the actor's preferred LSASS dumping technique: rundll32.exe loading comsvcs.dll's MiniDump export against the lsass.exe PID, often executed from a SYSTEM-level child of w3wp.exe or a scheduled task. Catches the very specific cmdline shape ('comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <out> full') that this actor reuses across intrusions even when they swap their webshell.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") Processes.process="*comsvcs*" Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*#24*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — comsvcs.dll MiniDump LSASS extraction
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","services.exe","svchost.exe")
| where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
          and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
          and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24",", 24"))
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
          and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
          and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (ProcessCommandLine has_all ("comsvcs.dll","MiniDump","full"))
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"MiniDump\s+(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine, TargetPid,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — comsvcs.dll MiniDump LSASS extraction (Sentinel via 4688 + Sysmon)
let ProcCreate =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where (Bin == "rundll32.exe"
               and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
               and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "#24" or CommandLine has ", 24"))
         or (Bin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
               and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
               and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump" and CommandLine has "full")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentBin, Bin, NewProcessName, CommandLine, Source = "4688";
let Sysmon1 =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 1
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             CommandLine = tostring(EventData.CommandLine),
             ParentImage = tostring(EventData.ParentImage),
             User = tostring(EventData.User)
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(Image, "\\")[-1])),
             ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentImage, "\\")[-1]))
    | where (Bin == "rundll32.exe"
               and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
               and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "#24" or CommandLine has ", 24"))
         or (Bin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
               and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
               and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump" and CommandLine has "full")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = User, ParentBin, Bin,
              NewProcessName = Image, CommandLine, Source = "Sysmon1";
union ProcCreate, Sysmon1
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 6c2f1e90-7c3d-4f5e-9b18-haf-comsvcs-1
status: stable
description: Detects rundll32 / powershell loading comsvcs.dll MiniDump export against an LSASS PID — Silk Typhoon's signature credential-theft technique post-ProxyLogon foothold.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_rundll:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs'
      - 'MiniDump'
  selection_rundll_ord:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'comsvcs.dll,#24'
      - 'comsvcs.dll, 24'
      - 'comsvcs.dll #24'
  selection_powershell:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs'
      - 'MiniDump'
  condition: 1 of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Internal red-team / forensic memory acquisition
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's Hafnium write-up and follow-on Silk Typhoon (2024-2025) reporting explicitly named the comsvcs.dll MiniDump pattern (`rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <lsass_pid> <dump> full`) as the actor's go-to credential-theft step after dropping a webshell on Exchange. The string 'comsvcs' + 'MiniDump' on the same command line is essentially never benign in production enviro
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
